L3.instruction:
Complete a professional pet portrait editing workflow on the image located at /home/user/Desktop/dog_with_background.png:

1. Remove the background using the 'Select by Color' tool (tolerance 25) to select the light blue water background, then invert the selection to keep only the dog, and delete the background to make it transparent.

2. Create a new layer named 'Gradient_BG' below the dog layer, fill it with a radial gradient from dark blue (#1a237e) at the center to purple (#4a148c) at the edges.

3. Adjust the dog layer's colors: increase saturation by 20%, increase contrast by 15%, and apply a slight warm color balance (+10 yellow).

4. Add a soft drop shadow to the dog layer: offset 8px, blur radius 15px, opacity 60%, color black.

5. Create a new layer named 'Vignette' at the top, fill with transparent black radial gradient (stronger at corners), and set layer opacity to 30%.

6. Add a text layer at the bottom center with 'HUSKY' in white, font size 48px, bold, with a subtle outer glow effect (white, 5px radius).

7. Export the final image as 'husky_portrait.png' with these specifications: PNG format with transparency, 1920px width (maintain aspect ratio), compression level 9. Also save the project as 'husky_project.xcf' preserving all layers.

L3.reason: 7 步骤的专业编辑工作流，涉及图层管理、色彩调整、文字叠加、导出等综合操作
L3.instruction:
Complete a verifiable portrait editing workflow using the image at /home/user/Desktop/woman_sitting_by_the_tree.png. Each step produces measurable, verifiable outputs:

1. Use the Free Select tool (Lasso) to draw a rough selection around the woman and her chair. Copy the selection to a new layer named exactly 'Subject', then hide the original background layer by clicking the eye icon.

2. Create a new layer named exactly 'Solid_BG' below the 'Subject' layer. Fill this layer with solid color #1a237e (dark blue) using the Bucket Fill tool.

3. Create a new layer named exactly 'Warm_Overlay' above the 'Subject' layer. Fill it with solid color #ff9966 (orange), set the layer blending mode to 'Overlay', and set opacity to exactly 25%.

4. Use the Brightness-Contrast adjustment on the 'Subject' layer: increase Brightness by exactly +20 and Contrast by exactly +30.

5. Create a new layer named exactly 'Vignette' at the top of the layer stack. Use the Elliptical Select tool to create an oval selection from center, then apply 'Select > Feather' with 100px radius, invert selection, and fill with black. Set layer opacity to exactly 40%.

6. Add three text layers with exact specifications:
   - Layer name: 'Text_Title', content: 'FASHION', font size 48px, color #ffffff (white), position at top-center
   - Layer name: 'Text_Subtitle', content: 'WEEKLY', font size 36px, color #ffffff (white), position below 'FASHION'
   - Layer name: 'Text_Year', content: '2024', font size 24px, color #ffcc00 (gold), position at bottom-right

7. Export the image as 'portrait_final.jpg' with exact specifications: JPEG format, width 1600 pixels (height auto), quality 90. Save the project as 'portrait_edit.xcf' with all layers preserved.

L3.instruction:
Using the triangle image at /home/user/Desktop/c5cc3472-253a-52d9-9982-701b77a50e9b_flat_image.png, complete a geometric logo design workflow:

1. Use the 'Select by Color' tool to select the yellow triangle, then copy it to a new layer named 'Original_Triangle'. Hide the original background layer.

2. Duplicate the 'Original_Triangle' layer 3 times, naming them 'Triangle_Red', 'Triangle_Blue', and 'Triangle_Green'. Change each triangle's color accordingly using the Colorize tool (Red: hue 0, Blue: hue 240, Green: hue 120), maintaining 80% saturation.

3. Apply transformations to create a dynamic composition:
   - Rotate 'Triangle_Red' by 45 degrees clockwise
   - Scale 'Triangle_Blue' to 70% of its original size
   - Flip 'Triangle_Green' horizontally

4. Arrange the three colored triangles using the Move tool:
   - Position 'Triangle_Red' at the top center
   - Position 'Triangle_Blue' at bottom left
   - Position 'Triangle_Green' at bottom right
   - Ensure they form a triangular composition with 50px spacing between them

5. Add a subtle drop shadow to all three triangle layers: 5px offset, 10px blur, 40% opacity, black color.

6. Create a new layer named 'Background_Gradient' at the bottom of the layer stack. Fill it with a linear gradient from dark gray (#2c3e50) at the top to darker gray (#1a252f) at the bottom.

7. Add a text layer named 'Logo_Text' at the center-bottom with 'TRIAD' in white, font size 36px, bold, letter spacing +2px. Apply a slight drop shadow (2px offset, 4px blur, 50% opacity).

8. Export the final design as 'triad_logo.png' (PNG format, 1200x1200px canvas, transparent background disabled), and save the project as 'triad_design.xcf' with all layers intact.

L3.instruction:
Complete a verifiable landscape photo enhancement workflow using the image at /home/user/Desktop/camera-man-donkey-5431685_1920.jpg. Each step produces measurable, verifiable outputs:

1. Create a new layer named exactly 'Sky_Enhance' above the background. Use the Free Select tool to select the sky area (top 35% of the image). Apply Hue-Saturation adjustment: increase Blue saturation by exactly +35 and Lightness by exactly -15 to make the sky more vibrant and dramatic.

2. Create a new layer named exactly 'Mountain_Contrast' above 'Sky_Enhance'. Use the Curves adjustment to create an S-curve: darken shadows by 20% and brighten highlights by 15% to enhance the mountain details.

3. Create a new layer named exactly 'Donkey_Warmth' above 'Mountain_Contrast'. Use the Color Balance adjustment on the donkey and foreground grass area: add +20 to Red channel and +15 to Yellow channel to create a warm golden hour effect.

4. Create a new layer named exactly 'Vignette' at the top of the layer stack. Use the Elliptical Select tool to create an oval covering 75% of the image from center, apply Select > Feather with 80px radius, invert selection, and fill with black at 25% opacity.

5. Add a text layer named exactly 'Text_Title' at the top-left corner with these exact specifications:
   - Content: 'ALPINE DREAM'
   - Font size: 36px
   - Color: #ffffff (white)
   - Position: 30px from left edge, 30px from top edge
   - Layer name: exactly 'Text_Title'

6. Add a text layer named exactly 'Text_Location' at the bottom-right corner with these exact specifications:
   - Content: 'SWISS ALPS'
   - Font size: 24px
   - Color: #ffd700 (gold)
   - Position: 30px from right edge, 30px from bottom edge
   - Layer name: exactly 'Text_Location'

7. Create a new layer named exactly 'Frame' at the very top. Use Select > All, then Edit > Stroke Selection with a 10px solid color #2f4f4f (dark slate gray) to create a border around the entire image.

8. Export the final image as 'alpine_masterpiece.jpg' with exact specifications: JPEG format, width 1600 pixels (height auto), quality 95. Save the project as 'alpine_project.xcf' with all layers preserved.
